side_effects.description: NAD+ and GHK-Cu both described as 'spicy' with potential pH-related tissue damage if pH too low

protocol_notes: Experienced vendor quality issues with NAD+ pH ranging 3-4 instead of optimal 5-6. Adds sodium bicarbonate Hospira BAC water to manage pH. Successfully injects NAD+ in stomach and thighs with proper pH management. GHK-Cu too spicy for stomach, uses glute injections. Uses pH strips to verify pH before injection. Hospira BAC water should be ~pH 5.
